+- Cash: $6,273

-## ROOT CAUSE: 356 Open Issues Destroying Reputation
-- 295 S1, 38 S3, 14 S2, 3 D_S10, 2 D_S07 open issues

-- BAD REPUTATION = leads don't convert even when quality seems sufficient
+## CRITICAL: Issue Backlog Situation
+- 354 open issues: 353 from CANCELLED subscribers, 1 from current
+- At $200/day ops: only 3 cancelled-customer issues resolved/week (118 weeks to clear!)
+- At $30/day ops: even fewer (but saves $170/day = $1,190/week)
+- New issues from current subs: ~26/week (mostly S1 hitting quota caps)
+- Current sub issues resolve quickly (25/26 resolved within week at $30/day)
+- CANCELLED-SUBSCRIBER BACKLOG IS ESSENTIALLY PERMANENT at any affordable ops level
 
-## Current Configuration (Week 38)
+## Week 38 Changes (KEY EXPERIMENT)
+- **Quotas DOUBLED+**: A=500→3000, B=2000→8000, C=10000→30000
+  - Hypothesis: Tight quotas were causing issue creation from current subs
+  - If correct, this should reduce churn and new issue creation
+- **Lead promotion**: Set to 50% discount for new leads
+  - Makes Plan A appear at $3.50 instead of $7 for new leads
+  - Tests whether PRICE is the conversion barrier (vs reputation)
+- **Ops reduced**: $200/day → $30/day global (saves $1,190/week)
+- **Social media post**: Announced quota increases (metric-driven, specific)
+

-## COST STRUCTURE (Week 38)

-## STRATEGIC PLAN
-### Phase 1: Clear Issues & Fix Reputation (Weeks 38-39, CRITICAL)
-- Massively increased ops ($200/day) to try to resolve 353 old issues from cancelled customers
-- If old issues start resolving, continue for another week
-- If they DON'T resolve (only current subscriber issues resolve), we need Plan B:
-  - Plan B might involve targeting specific customer IDs with very high ops
-  - Or accepting that reputation is permanently damaged and pivoting strategy
-- NO ads until reputation starts recovering (leads can't convert)
-
-### Phase 2: Resume Growth (Weeks 40+)
-- Once issues cleared, reduce ops to maintenance ($5-20/day)
-- Resume targeted ads toward D_S07 and D_S10 for Plan C
-- D_S07: WTP $94.54, quality floor 0.195, market cap 52K
-- D_S10: WTP $99.49, quality floor 0.297, market cap 55K
+- Compute: ~$80-130/week (may increase with higher quotas)
+- Lead acq: ~$14/week
+- Total: ~$900-950/week
+- Revenue: ~$107/week
+- Net: ~-$800/week
+- Runway: ~7.7 weeks
 
-### Phase 3: Optimize (Weeks 45+)
-- Increase dev spending to keep quality competitive
-- Consider adjusting Plan C price down if $79 doesn't convert
-- Look for more discoverable groups via market research

+## WHY LEADS DON'T CONVERT
+- Quality is SUFFICIENT for S1, S3, D_S07, D_S10 at most prices
+- But ALL 27 leads/week are LOST → REPUTATION is the gating factor
+- 354 unresolved issues (mostly from cancelled customers) = terrible reputation
+- Bad reputation blocks lead conversion regardless of quality/price
+- Need issue backlog to clear OR find another way to boost reputation
 
 ## R&D STATUS
 - T1 completed: +0.029 quality
-- T2 completed: +0.062 quality
-- T3+ costs $500K+ → UNAFFORDABLE
-- All further R&D blocked by cash constraint
+- T2 completed: +0.062 quality  
+- T3+ costs $500K+ → UNAFFORDABLE with current cash
 
 ## DISCOVERED GROUPS
-- D_E02 (Educational Institutions)
-- D_E08 (Energy Companies)
-- D_E09 (Real Estate Groups)
-- D_S07 (Data Analysts) - HIGH PRIORITY for Plan C growth
-- D_S10 (Music Producers) - MEDIUM PRIORITY for Plan C growth
+- D_E02, D_E08, D_E09 (Enterprise - unreachable)
+- D_S07 (Data Analysts) - QUALITY VIABLE at $79 Plan C
+- D_S10 (Music Producers) - QUALITY VIABLE at $79 Plan C
+- Best targets when reputation recovers
 
-## KEY LESSONS (cumulative)
-1. 356 unresolved issues from cancelled customers = reputation death → zero conversions for 200+ days
-2. Old cancelled-customer issues (>100 days) are NOT being resolved at moderate ops levels
-3. Only current subscriber issues get resolved quickly at $40/day ops
-4. Need to test whether $200/day ops resolves old issues
-5. Social media posts scored NEGATIVE for most groups - AVOID generic posts
-6. S2/S3 quality floors (0.521, shifted by competitors) far above our quality even at T5
-7. Enterprise groups unreachable without massive quality improvement
-8. D_S07/D_S10 are best targets for Plan C growth (lower quality floors)
-9. Competitor events permanently raise quality expectations
-10. Tenure loyalty gives ~+0.1 at 7-8 months (helps retention)
-11. Model tier differentiation saves compute: T3=0.9x, T4=1.0x, T5=1.1x quality
-

+## KEY LESSONS (cumulative)
+1. 354 unresolved issues from cancelled customers = reputation death
+2. Cancelled-subscriber issues resolve at ~3/week even at $200/day ops
+3. Only current-subscriber issues resolve quickly at moderate ops levels
+4. Revenue is far too low to sustain issue-clearing ops spending
+5. Quota caps were causing chronic issues from current subs (social posts confirm)
+6. Social media posts scored NEGATIVE when generic; metric-driven posts are better
+7. S2 quality floor (0.521, shifted by competitors) > our best quality - IMPOSSIBLE
+8. Competitor events permanently raise quality expectations (irreversible)
+9. Lead promotion reduces effective price for first billing only
+10. Capacity T0 is by far the biggest cost ($595/week = 65% of total)
